isolation.qemuFlags: virt,gic-version=2 cortex-a53 smp=4 256M with virtio-gpu, virtio-keyboard, virtio-9p font share

steps.action: Launch QEMU and wait for boot (~8s)
steps.expected: OS boots with title bar, clock, cursor, empty document area
steps.observed: Title bar with document icon 'Text', clock '13:08:11', white cursor at top-left of document area, dark background, shadow below title bar. All elements render cleanly.

steps.action: Take initial boot screenshot (before any typing)
steps.observed: Clean initial render — title bar, clock, cursor, and empty content area all correct. No stale pixels or corruption.

steps.action: Send 'hello world' keystrokes via monitor socket (11 keys with 0.3s delays)
steps.expected: Characters appear on screen with cursor advancing
steps.observed: All 11 characters ('hello world') rendered correctly in monospace font. Cursor positioned at end of text. No stale pixels from previous cursor positions.

steps.expected: Text 'hello world' visible, cursor at end, no artifacts
steps.observed: Text 'hello world' clearly visible. Clock updated to '13:08:38' (confirming incremental clock updates also work). Cursor at end of text. No visual artifacts.

steps.action: Send Enter + 'this is a test' keystrokes rapidly (0.1s delays) to stress partial damage
steps.expected: Second line appears with all characters, no corruption
steps.observed: Both lines render correctly: 'hello world' on line 1, 'this is a test' on line 2. Cursor at end of line 2. No ghosting or stale pixels between lines.

steps.expected: Two lines of text, cursor at end, no artifacts
steps.observed: Two lines rendered cleanly. Clock updated to '13:09:05'. Title bar, shadow, and all chrome elements intact. No stale pixels, no rendering corruption from partial damage updates.
Serial log clean — no panics, crashes, faults, or errors. Compositor confirmed 'entering render loop (damage-tracked)'.

evidence.serialLog: Clean — no errors, panics, or crashes. Compositor running in damage-tracked mode.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-COMP-004 (partial update renders correctly) confirmed — typed 'hello world' and 'this is a test' across two lines via QEMU monitor keystrokes. All characters rendered correctly with no stale pixels, visual artifacts, or rendering corruption. Title bar, clock (with incremental updates), cursor, and text area all render cleanly under partial damage tracking. Serial log shows no errors.
